company.name: BridgeBio Pharma
company.subtitle: Attruby $362M Y1 · ATTR-CM first-choice therapy · BBP-418 LGMD NDA H1 2026 · Genetic disease platform
company.cashRunway: Into profitability trajectory ($588M cash + growing Attruby revenue)

market.notes: Attruby 2025: $362M (Y1 US). Q4: $146M ($584M annualized). 7,804 unique Rx by Feb 2026. First near-complete (≥90%) TTR stabilizer. ATTRibute-CM: 49% hazard reduction in CVM/CVH at Month 30. 81% survival rate approaches age-matched population. Competes with tafamidis (Pfizer Vyndaqel $5B+). Differentiated: faster onset (1 month), better TTR stabilization. Global launches underway.

FORTIFY Phase 3: 2.6pt benefit on NSAD (p<α), broad benefit across subgroups. FDA recommended traditional full approval NDA. NDA submission H1 2026. Ultra-rare neuromuscular disease. Orphan pricing expected ($200-400K/yr).
Low-dose infigratinib Phase 3 positive for achondroplasia (most common form of dwarfism). Competes with Voxzogo (BioMarin, $500M+). Differentiated on dosing convenience. NDA/MAA submissions planned.
CaSR inhibitor for ADH1. Phase 3 positive. NDA/MAA submissions planned. Ultra-rare disease — orphan pricing. Also exploring chronic hypoparathyroidism.

assets.name: TTR amyloid depleter antibody + combo pipeline

market.notes: New depleter antibody program to reverse ATTR amyloid deposits (not just stabilize). Clinic 2027-2028. If it works: Attruby (stabilize) + depleter (reverse) = complete ATTR-CM franchise. Also oral GLP-1 combo for potential expansion.

mega_bear.narrative: Attruby loses to tafamidis on price/access + pipeline delivers only niche orphans
events: ✗ Payers restrict Attruby in favor of generic tafamidis (2030s)
✗ BBP-418 LGMD, infigratinib, encaleret all approved but tiny markets
✗ TTR depleter fails in preclinical — Attruby alone insufficient
→ Attruby peak $2-3B only, pipeline adds $500M, overvalued at $15B

bear.narrative: Attruby grows but shares ATTR-CM with tafamidis + orphan revenue small
events: ✓ Attruby grows to $2B but tafamidis retains majority share
✓ LGMD + achondroplasia + ADH1 approved — combined $500M peak
~ Depleter program slow to advance
→ Total peak $3B — $15B market cap = 5x peak, fair but no upside

base.narrative: Attruby becomes ATTR-CM standard of care + 3 rare disease launches + depleter advances
events: ✓ Attruby $4B+ peak — displaces tafamidis as first-line in ATTR-CM
✓ BBP-418, infigratinib, encaleret all approved — rare disease franchise
✓ TTR depleter enters clinic — disease reversal potential
→ $5B+ peak revenue across franchise, approaching profitability

bull.narrative: ATTR franchise dominance (stabilize + deplete) + rare disease portfolio + $8B+ peak
events: ✓ Attruby $6B+ — dominant globally in ATTR-CM, displaces tafamidis
✓ Depleter antibody works — Attruby + depleter = complete disease modification
✓ 4+ approved rare disease products — genetic disease platform validated
✓ Revenue $8B+, highly profitable, M&A target

psychedelic_bull.narrative: ATTR-CM cured + rare disease franchise + Roche/Novartis acquires at $150+
events: ✓ Attruby + depleter = functional cure for ATTR-CM — $10B+ franchise
✓ 5+ approved rare disease medicines — BridgeBio model validated
✓ Platform generates next wave of genetic disease therapies
✓ Roche or Novartis acquires for $30-40B to own genetic disease platform

lastData.title: Attruby $362M Y1 + 7,804 Rx + 3 NDA-track programs
points: 7,804 unique patient Rx by Feb 2026, 1,856 prescribers
ATTRibute-CM OLE: survival + disease stabilization data at ACC 2026
BBP-418 LGMD: FORTIFY positive, NDA H1 2026 (traditional approval)
Infigratinib achondroplasia: Phase 3 positive, NDA planned
Encaleret ADH1: Phase 3 positive, NDA planned
Ervogastat (DGAT-2, licensed from Pfizer): Phase 2 combo planned

nextCatalyst.title: BBP-418 LGMD NDA submission + Attruby revenue trajectory

risks: $15B market cap with $362M revenue — priced for perfection
Tafamidis (Pfizer Vyndaqel) has massive installed base + first-mover advantage
ATTR-CM market share split uncertain — will Attruby truly displace tafamidis?
BBP-418/infigratinib/encaleret are ultra-rare — small revenue individually
Still loss-making despite $362M revenue — SG&A for launch is high
Debt on balance sheet from prior years
TTR depleter is preclinical — years from clinical proof
Alnylam patisiran + Ionis eplontersen competing in ATTR space
